secondary_archetype: Technical PM focused on enterprise/industry verticals (0-to-1 products in regulated industries)

level_signals: Expected to own multi-year product strategy and influence core platform teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years of product management experience, with a track record of shipping successful products in complex, regulated environments.

Technical fluency—You don't need to write code, but you're comfortable reading an API spec, reasoning about system design trade-offs, and holding your own in engineering discussions. You've shipped products where the "how we build it" mattered as much as the "what."
Deep customer obsession—A talent for getting close to customers—talking to merchants and partners—and distilling complex pain points into simple, elegant product requirements.

Analytical rigor—You use data—not just intuition—to prioritize. You're comfortable defining metrics frameworks, digging into usage data, and making quantitative arguments for (or against) product investments.

A master communicator—Stripe is a writing-heavy culture. You must be able to articulate deep technical and strategic concepts through precise, persuasive prose—your narrative docs are things of beauty.
High agency and influence—Adept at finding "homes" for initiatives, building consensus across disparate teams, and driving momentum without needing formal authority.
Entrepreneurial mindset and bias for action—Comfortable with ambiguity, skilled at prioritizing high-impact initiatives, and experienced operating with limited resources to unlock outsized outcomes.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct experience building products in Healthtech, Insurtech, Public Sector, or similarly complex, regulated industries.
Experience building platform, API, or developer-facing products.
Experience working closely with Enterprise Sales teams and C-suite stakeholders at Fortune 500 companies.

scope.surface: Enterprise industry verticals (insurance, healthcare, public sector); industry-specific 0-to-1 products and platform influence across Payments, Billing, Connect
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise; financial infrastructure platform (payments and revenue growth)
core_responsibilities: Own the industry product roadmap—Define and execute the multi-year product strategy for your priority vertical.
Ship 0→1 products—Identify unmet needs through deep customer research, data analysis, and market intelligence.
Be a strategic partner to GTM and customers—Work deeply with our largest customers, partners, and GTM teams.

Act as a force multiplier across Stripe's platform—Influence Stripe's core product teams (Payments, Billing, Connect, and beyond) to ensure their roadmaps reflect the requirements of enterprise customers in your vertical.

green_flags: Clear ownership model — PM acts as 'CEO' of assigned vertical with measurable outcomes
Explicit 0-to-1 mandate with real product lifecycle ownership
High-impact, strategic role directly tied to one of Stripe's most strategic segments
Preferred qualifications clearly labeled as bonuses, not requirements, encouraging broader applicant pool
Minimum requirements articulated precisely with specific year threshold (12+)
red_flags: No compensation range disclosed anywhere in the JD
Location and work authorization not stated — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
12+ years minimum is a very high bar that may screen out strong Staff-level candidates
Archetype is Generalist/Technical PM but listed under 'AI Product Manager' enum — no AI-specific content in JD
